label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. The story is about Mr. Williams getting stopped by a policeman even though he thought he was following the rules. The question asks what he was doing when he was stopped. First, let me recap the key points from the story. The crossroads had a "SLOW" sign, not a "STOP" sign. Mr. Williams slowed down as he approached the main road, looked both ways, and then drove across without completely stopping. As a result, the policeman stopped him and said he didn't stop at the crossing. Mr. Williams argued that the sign only said "SLOW," and he did go slow. Then the policeman realized he was in the wrong street. Now, looking at the question: When he was stopped by a policeman, he ___. Options are: A) was driving at a high speed B) was driving onto the main road C) was going to stop his car D) was driving slowly Let's analyze each option in the context of the story. A) was driving at a high speed: This is unlikely because the story says he "slowed down" when he came to the main road, so he was not driving at high speed. B) was driving onto the main road: The story says he drove across without stopping completely. So, he was driving onto the main road after slowing down.
